objects.title: Todo App

source.task: Curated public Todo App demo seed

objects.body:
# Todo App

A small personal productivity app for capturing tasks quickly, organizing them by status, and reviewing what needs attention today.

The project should stay understandable to any developer opening the public demo.

objects.title: Product intent

source.task: Curated public Todo App demo seed

objects.body:
# Product intent

The Todo App helps a single user keep a trustworthy short list of work without account setup or network dependency.

Useful output means fast capture, clear completion state, and no surprise data loss after refresh.

objects.title: Feature map

source.task: Curated public Todo App demo seed

objects.body:
# Feature map

- Quick add creates a task from a single text input.
- Filtered views separate all, active, completed, and overdue tasks.
- Local persistence keeps tasks available after reload.
- Empty states explain the current filter without sending the user to documentation.

objects.title: Repository map

source.task: Curated public Todo App demo seed

objects.body:
# Repository map

Application code lives under `src/`, reusable UI pieces live under `src/components/`, and persistence helpers live under `src/storage/`.

Tests mirror those areas under `test/` with browser coverage for task creation and filtering.

objects.title: Local-first todo architecture

source.task: Curated public Todo App demo seed

objects.body:
# Local-first todo architecture

The app keeps task state in a small client-side store and persists a normalized task list to localStorage.

Rendering code reads from the store; persistence code only serializes and validates task records.

objects.title: Stack and tooling

source.task: Curated public Todo App demo seed

objects.body:
# Stack and tooling

The demo Todo App uses TypeScript, Vite, and Vitest. Browser behavior is covered with Playwright-style flow tests.

Package scripts are the preferred interface for build, test, and lint checks.

objects.title: Conventions and quality bar

source.task: Curated public Todo App demo seed

objects.body:
# Conventions and quality bar

Keep task mutations explicit, preserve keyboard access for every task action, and keep empty states tied to the active filter.

Avoid adding accounts, sync, or collaboration features unless the product scope changes.

objects.title: Stay offline first

source.task: Curated public Todo App demo seed

objects.body:
# Stay offline first

Task creation, completion, filtering, and persistence must work without a backend service.

Do not add a hosted database or login flow to satisfy demo requirements.

objects.title: Quick add

source.task: Curated public Todo App demo seed

objects.body:
# Quick add

Quick add accepts a non-empty task title, trims whitespace, creates an active task, and clears the input after save.

objects.title: Filtered task views

source.task: Curated public Todo App demo seed

objects.body:
# Filtered task views

The list supports all, active, completed, and overdue views. Counts must match the same predicate used to render each view.

objects.title: LocalStorage persistence

source.task: Curated public Todo App demo seed

objects.body:
# LocalStorage persistence

The task store writes a JSON array of task records to localStorage and ignores malformed saved records on load.

objects.title: Local development workflow

source.task: Curated public Todo App demo seed

objects.body:
# Local development workflow

Run `pnpm install`, `pnpm dev`, and then open the Vite URL. Use the browser flow before changing persistence behavior.

objects.title: Post-task verification

source.task: Curated public Todo App demo seed

objects.body:
# Post-task verification

Before finishing Todo App work, run `pnpm test`, `pnpm run typecheck`, and a browser check for add, complete, filter, and reload behavior.

objects.title: Completed filter counts can drift

source.task: Curated public Todo App demo seed

objects.body:
# Completed filter counts can drift

Do not compute filter badges with a different predicate than the rendered list. This caused completed tasks to appear in the active count before.

objects.title: Recurring task scope

source.task: Curated public Todo App demo seed

objects.body:
# Recurring task scope

Should recurring tasks be part of the Todo App, or should the app stay focused on one-off personal tasks for the first release?

objects.title: Agent guidance

source.task: Curated public Todo App demo seed

objects.body:
# Agent guidance

Load Memory before non-trivial Todo App work. Preserve the offline-first scope, keep UI behavior keyboard accessible, and verify persistence after reload.

source.task: Curated public Todo App demo seed

objects.body:
# Source: docs/product-brief.md

The product brief describes a local-first Todo App for quick capture, filtering, and dependable reload behavior.

source.task: Curated public Todo App demo seed

objects.body:
# Source: package.json

The package manifest records TypeScript, Vite, Vitest, and the package scripts used for local development and verification.

source.task: Curated public Todo App demo seed

objects.body:
# Source: README.md

The README explains the Todo App repository layout, local development path, and browser behavior users should expect.

objects.title: Source: AGENTS.md

source.task: Curated public Todo App demo seed

objects.body:
# Source: AGENTS.md

Agent instructions require loading memory before substantial Todo App changes and saving only durable project knowledge.

lenses.name: project-map
lenses.title: Project Map
lenses.markdown:
# Project Map

Default overview for purpose, capabilities, layout, stack, and operating norms.

## Role coverage

- Product Intent: populated
- Capability Map: populated
- Repository Map: populated
- Architecture / Patterns: populated
- Stack / Tooling: populated
- Conventions / Quality Bar: populated
- Workflows / How-tos: populated
- Verification: populated
- Gotchas / Risks: populated
- Open Questions: populated
- Agent Guidance: populated

## Local-first todo architecture

Memory: `architecture.local-first-todo-app` (architecture, active)

The app keeps task state in a small client-side store and persists a normalized task list to localStorage.

Rendering code reads from the store; persistence code only serializes and validates task records.

## Filtered task views

Memory: `concept.filtered-views` (concept, active)

The list supports all, active, completed, and overdue views. Counts must match the same predicate used to render each view.

## Quick add

Memory: `concept.quick-add` (concept, active)

Quick add accepts a non-empty task title, trims whitespace, creates an active task, and clears the input after save.

## Stay offline first

Memory: `constraint.offline-first` (constraint, active)

Task creation, completion, filtering, and persistence must work without a backend service.

Do not add a hosted database or login flow to satisfy demo requirements.

## Completed filter counts can drift

Memory: `gotcha.completed-filter-counts` (gotcha, active)

Do not compute filter badges with a different predicate than the rendered list. This caused completed tasks to appear in the active count before.

## Recurring task scope

Memory: `question.recurring-tasks` (question, open)

Should recurring tasks be part of the Todo App, or should the app stay focused on one-off personal tasks for the first release?

## Agent guidance

Memory: `synthesis.agent-guidance` (synthesis, active)

Load Memory before non-trivial Todo App work. Preserve the offline-first scope, keep UI behavior keyboard accessible, and verify persistence after reload.

## Conventions and quality bar

Memory: `synthesis.conventions-quality` (synthesis, active)

Keep task mutations explicit, preserve keyboard access for every task action, and keep empty states tied to the active filter.

Avoid adding accounts, sync, or collaboration features unless the product scope changes.

## Feature map

Memory: `synthesis.feature-map` (synthesis, active)

- Quick add creates a task from a single text input.
- Filtered views separate all, active, completed, and overdue tasks.
- Local persistence keeps tasks available after reload.
- Empty states explain the current filter without sending the user to documentation.

## Product intent

Memory: `synthesis.product-intent` (synthesis, active)

The Todo App helps a single user keep a trustworthy short list of work without account setup or network dependency.

Useful output means fast capture, clear completion state, and no surprise data loss after refresh.

## Repository map

Memory: `synthesis.repository-map` (synthesis, active)

Application code lives under `src/`, reusable UI pieces live under `src/components/`, and persistence helpers live under `src/storage/`.

Tests mirror those areas under `test/` with browser coverage for task creation and filtering.

## Stack and tooling

Memory: `synthesis.stack-and-tooling` (synthesis, active)

The demo Todo App uses TypeScript, Vite, and Vitest. Browser behavior is covered with Playwright-style flow tests.

Package scripts are the preferred interface for build, test, and lint checks.

## Local development workflow

Memory: `workflow.local-development` (workflow, active)

Run `pnpm install`, `pnpm dev`, and then open the Vite URL. Use the browser flow before changing persistence behavior.

## Post-task verification

Memory: `workflow.post-task-verification` (workflow, active)

Before finishing Todo App work, run `pnpm test`, `pnpm run typecheck`, and a browser check for add, complete, filter, and reload behavior.

## Relation context

- `project.todo-app` implements `concept.quick-add` (active)
- `project.todo-app` implements `concept.filtered-views` (active)
- `synthesis.product-intent` summarizes `project.todo-app` (active)
- `synthesis.feature-map` documents `project.todo-app` (active)
- `synthesis.repository-map` documents `project.todo-app` (active)
- `architecture.local-first-todo-app` documents `project.todo-app` (active)
- `synthesis.stack-and-tooling` documents `project.todo-app` (active)
- `synthesis.conventions-quality` documents `project.todo-app` (active)
- `synthesis.agent-guidance` documents `project.todo-app` (active)
- `workflow.local-development` supports `project.todo-app` (active)
- `workflow.post-task-verification` supports `project.todo-app` (active)
- `constraint.offline-first` affects `project.todo-app` (active)
- `synthesis.product-intent` derived_from `source.product-brief` (active)
- `synthesis.feature-map` derived_from `source.product-brief` (active)
- `question.recurring-tasks` challenges `synthesis.feature-map` (active)
- `synthesis.feature-map` summarizes `concept.quick-add` (active)
- `synthesis.feature-map` summarizes `concept.filtered-views` (active)
- `synthesis.repository-map` derived_from `source.readme` (active)
- `architecture.local-first-todo-app` derived_from `source.readme` (active)
- `synthesis.stack-and-tooling` derived_from `source.package-json` (active)
- `synthesis.conventions-quality` derived_from `source.product-brief` (active)
- `workflow.local-development` derived_from `source.package-json` (active)
- `workflow.post-task-verification` derived_from `source.package-json` (active)
- `constraint.offline-first` derived_from `source.product-brief` (active)
- `gotcha.completed-filter-counts` affects `concept.filtered-views` (active)
- `concept.filtered-views` depends_on `fact.storage-localstorage` (active)
- `synthesis.agent-guidance` derived_from `source.agent-guidance` (active)
- `constraint.offline-first` requires `fact.storage-localstorage` (active)

Memory: `synthesis.product-intent` (synthesis, active)

The Todo App helps a single user keep a trustworthy short list of work without account setup or network dependency.

Useful output means fast capture, clear completion state, and no surprise data loss after refresh.

## Repository map

Memory: `synthesis.repository-map` (synthesis, active)

Application code lives under `src/`, reusable UI pieces live under `src/components/`, and persistence helpers live under `src/storage/`.

Tests mirror those areas under `test/` with browser coverage for task creation and filtering.

## Stack and tooling

Memory: `synthesis.stack-and-tooling` (synthesis, active)

The demo Todo App uses TypeScript, Vite, and Vitest. Browser behavior is covered with Playwright-style flow tests.

Package scripts are the preferred interface for build, test, and lint checks.

## Local development workflow

Memory: `workflow.local-development` (workflow, active)

Run `pnpm install`, `pnpm dev`, and then open the Vite URL. Use the browser flow before changing persistence behavior.

## Post-task verification

Memory: `workflow.post-task-verification` (workflow, active)

Before finishing Todo App work, run `pnpm test`, `pnpm run typecheck`, and a browser check for add, complete, filter, and reload behavior.

roles.label: Product Intent
roles.description: What this project is for and what useful output means.

roles.label: Capability Map
roles.description: Features, outputs, commands, APIs, or generation capabilities.

roles.label: Repository Map
roles.description: Where important code, docs, tests, assets, and configs live.

roles.label: Architecture / Patterns
roles.description: How the project is organized and which design patterns matter.

roles.label: Stack / Tooling
roles.description: Languages, frameworks, package managers, build tools, and runtime constraints.

roles.label: Conventions / Quality Bar
roles.description: Project-specific coding, design, review, and quality expectations.

roles.label: Workflows / How-tos
roles.description: Reusable setup, release, debugging, migration, generation, or maintenance procedures.

roles.label: Verification
roles.description: Commands and checks agents should run before claiming work is done.

roles.label: Gotchas / Risks
roles.description: Known traps, fragile areas, abandoned approaches, or recurring failure modes.

roles.label: Open Questions
roles.description: Important unknowns agents should not guess.

roles.label: Sources / Provenance
roles.description: Source records and provenance relations backing durable memory.

roles.label: Agent Guidance
roles.description: Project-specific operating rules for AI coding agents.
